clinical_context: Neonatal heart rate per PALS 2020-2025 guidelines; includes sleep/wake variation
Infant heart rate per PALS 2020-2025 and Iowa Protocols 2025
Toddler heart rate per PALS 2020-2025; includes sleep/wake variation
Preschool heart rate per PALS 2020-2025 guidelines
School-age heart rate per PALS 2020-2025 guidelines
Adolescent heart rate per PALS 2020-2025 guidelines
Adult resting heart rate per AHA 2025 guidelines; lower in trained athletes
Neonatal respiratory rate per PALS 2020-2025; higher rates normal
Infant respiratory rate per PALS 2020-2025 guidelines
Toddler respiratory rate per PALS 2020-2025 guidelines
Preschool respiratory rate per PALS 2020-2025 guidelines
School-age respiratory rate per PALS 2020-2025 guidelines
Adolescent respiratory rate per PALS 2020-2025 guidelines
Adult respiratory rate; >30/min indicates acute respiratory failure in COPD
Neonatal temperature; critical for thermoregulation assessment
Pediatric temperature; fever >38°C (100.4°F) requires evaluation
Adult temperature; fever >38°C (100.4°F); hypothermia <35°C
Oxygen saturation at sea level on room air; <92% requires evaluation, <88% severe hypoxemia
Infant systolic BP per Iowa Protocols 2025
Toddler systolic BP per Iowa Protocols 2025
Preschool systolic BP; AAP screening begins at age 3
School-age systolic BP; percentile-based assessment required per AAP 2017
Adolescent systolic BP; Elevated: 120-129, Stage 1 HTN: 130-139, Stage 2: ≥140 (AAP 2017 simplified thresholds)
Adult systolic BP; Normal: <120, Elevated: 120-129, Stage 1 HTN: 130-139, Stage 2 HTN: ≥140 (AHA/ACC 2025)
Infant diastolic BP per Iowa Protocols 2025
Toddler diastolic BP per Iowa Protocols 2025
Preschool diastolic BP per Iowa Protocols 2025
School-age diastolic BP per Iowa Protocols; percentile-based assessment per AAP 2017
Adolescent diastolic BP; Stage 1 HTN: 80-89, Stage 2: ≥90 (AAP 2017)
Adult diastolic BP; Normal: <80, Stage 1 HTN: 80-89, Stage 2: ≥90 (AHA/ACC 2025)
Fasting blood glucose; Normal: 70-100, Prediabetes: 100-125, Diabetes: ≥126 mg/dL (ADA 2025)
Gestational diabetes fasting target <95 mg/dL; requires multiple daily measurements (ADA 2025)
Pregnancy systolic BP; Alert: ≥140 mmHg, Urgent: ≥160 mmHg (ACOG Practice Bulletin 222)
Pregnancy diastolic BP; Alert: ≥90 mmHg, Urgent: ≥110 mmHg (ACOG Practice Bulletin 222)
Hypertension management target <130 mmHg systolic; home BP monitoring recommended (AHA/ACC 2025)
Hypertension management target <80 mmHg diastolic (AHA/ACC 2025)
CKD BP target <120 mmHg systolic using standardized office measurement (KDIGO 2021)
CKD diastolic BP; home BP monitoring recommended (KDIGO 2021)
COPD oxygen saturation target 88-92% on supplemental oxygen during exacerbations; <92% off oxygen requires ABG (GOLD 2025)
Non-diabetic A1c range; Prediabetes: 5.7-6.4%, Diabetes: ≥6.5% (ADA 2025)
Type 1 & 2 diabetes A1c target <7% for most nonpregnant adults; <8% for elderly or complicated cases (ADA 2025)
Gestational diabetes A1c target <6% in second/third trimesters if achievable without significant hypoglycemia (ADA 2025)
Adult BMI classification per WHO 2023: Underweight <18.5, Normal 18.5-24.9, Overweight 25-29.9, Obese Class I 30-34.9, Class II 35-39.9, Class III ≥40
Pediatric BMI (5-18 years) requires age/sex-specific percentiles for classification; this range captures 5th-95th percentiles (WHO 2023)
Mean Arterial Pressure (MAP) for adults; MAP <60 indicates inadequate organ perfusion, >100 indicates hypertension risk (AHA 2025)
Male neonate head circumference (3rd-97th percentile); microcephaly <32cm, macrocephaly >38cm (WHO Child Growth Standards 2023)
Female neonate head circumference (3rd-97th percentile); microcephaly <31.5cm, macrocephaly >37.5cm (WHO Child Growth Standards 2023)
Male infant head circumference 1-12 months (3rd-97th percentile); critical for brain development monitoring (WHO 2023)
Female infant head circumference 1-12 months (3rd-97th percentile); critical for brain development monitoring (WHO 2023)
Male toddler head circumference 1-3 years (3rd-97th percentile); measured until age 3 per WHO standards
Female toddler head circumference 1-3 years (3rd-97th percentile); measured until age 3 per WHO standards
MUAC for malnutrition screening 3 months-5 years: <11.5cm severe acute malnutrition (SAM), 11.5-12.5cm moderate acute malnutrition (MAM), ≥12.5cm normal (WHO 2023)
Male triceps skinfold thickness 3 months-5 years for nutritional assessment; <5th percentile indicates undernutrition (WHO 2023)
Female triceps skinfold thickness 3 months-5 years for nutritional assessment; <5th percentile indicates undernutrition (WHO 2023)
Male neonate birth weight (3rd-97th percentile); <2.5kg low birth weight, >4.5kg macrosomia (WHO 2023)
Female neonate birth weight (3rd-97th percentile); <2.4kg low birth weight, >4.2kg macrosomia (WHO 2023)
Male infant weight 1-12 months (3rd-97th percentile); rapid growth expected, double birth weight by 5 months (WHO 2023)
Female infant weight 1-12 months (3rd-97th percentile); rapid growth expected (WHO 2023)
Male toddler weight 1-3 years (3rd-97th percentile); steady growth ~2kg/year expected (WHO 2023)
Female toddler weight 1-3 years (3rd-97th percentile); steady growth expected (WHO 2023)
Heart failure daily weight monitoring: Alert for 2-3 lbs (0.9-1.4kg) gain in 1 day or 5+ lbs (2.3kg) in 1 week indicating fluid retention (ACC/AHA/HFSA 2022)
Male neonate birth length (3rd-97th percentile); measured supine as "length" until age 2 (WHO 2023)
Female neonate birth length (3rd-97th percentile); measured supine as "length" until age 2 (WHO 2023)
Male infant length 1-12 months (3rd-97th percentile); rapid linear growth in first year (WHO 2023)
Female infant length 1-12 months (3rd-97th percentile); rapid linear growth in first year (WHO 2023)
Male toddler length 1-3 years (3rd-97th percentile); transition to standing height measurement at age 2 (WHO 2023)
Female toddler length 1-3 years (3rd-97th percentile); transition to standing height measurement at age 2 (WHO 2023)
Male height 3-18 years (3rd-97th percentile); steady growth with adolescent growth spurt; required for BP percentile calculation (WHO 2023, AAP 2017)
Female height 3-18 years (3rd-97th percentile); steady growth with adolescent growth spurt; required for BP percentile calculation (WHO 2023, AAP 2017)
Adult male height; used for BMI calculation and nutritional assessment (WHO 2023)
Adult female height; used for BMI calculation and nutritional assessment (WHO 2023)
